majors.count_method: counted extracted undergraduate-major titles from an official page
majors.notes: auto-enrichment could not derive a reliable major/program count from the pages reached in this pass; titles extracted from official page: https://www.umass.edu/gateway/academics/accelerated-masters-4plus1-degree

admissions.testing_policy: UMass Amherst is test-optional for first-year applicants and reviews applications with or without standardized test scores across all majors.
admissions.gpa_policy: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.

evidence.claim: Think Big. Act Together. Be the Future.
evidence.source_excerpt: Think Big. Act Together. Be the Future.

evidence.claim: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.
evidence.source_excerpt: No explicit minimum GPA was found on the cited official pages used in this auto-enrichment pass.

evidence.claim: The Pre-College programs at UMass Amherst offer exceptional opportunities for high school students to experience the rigor of collegiate academics and earn college credit within an international community of their peers. With opportunities to extend their learning through summer programs or even in undergraduate classrooms alongside other highly-motivated students, the possibilities to expand your horizons are limitless.
evidence.source_excerpt: The Pre-College programs at UMass Amherst offer exceptional opportunities for high school students to experience the rigor of collegiate academics and earn college credit within an international community of their peers. With opportunities to extend their learning through summer programs or even in undergraduate classrooms alongside other highly-motivated students, the possibilities to expand your horizons are limitless.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 5 professors from RateMyProfessors by rating count and sentiment metrics.
evidence.source_excerpt: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'University of Massachusetts - Amherst' (legacyId=1513) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

evidence.claim: Ranked top 10 notable alumni from Wikipedia-derived candidate lists using trailing pageviews.
evidence.source_excerpt: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, then ranked by pageviews.

evidence.claim: UMass Amherst is test-optional for first-year applicants and reviews applications with or without standardized test scores across all majors.
evidence.source_excerpt: At UMass Amherst, standardized tests are optional for first-year entering applicants. UMass Amherst will review applications with or without standardized test scores. The test-optional approach at UMass Amherst applies to all majors.

warnings: Major titles extracted from official school source (34 titles).
Phase 5 auto-enrichment completed using official-school web crawl heuristics; manual spot-checking is still recommended for edge cases.
GPA policy was normalized from absence of an explicit minimum-GPA statement on the cited official pages.
Majors count needs manual confirmation; the crawler did not find a clean count or stable program-list page.
School-people enrichment uses public third-party sources (RateMyProfessors and Wikipedia/Wikimedia), so rankings are heuristic rather than official university data.
admissions.course_rigor was reset to unknown during manual cleanup because the earlier auto-extracted text was unrelated to the requested admissions field.
UMass Amherst testing policy was manually repaired from the official admissions test-optional policy page after the auto-crawl captured a slogan.

popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'University of Massachusetts - Amherst' (legacyId=1513) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Wikipedia list/category candidates ranked by trailing-365-day Wikipedia pageviews.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Candidates were harvested from school-specific Wikipedia alumni/people pages or categories, filtered to likely people, then ranked by pageviews.
